# @spkm/ui - Full LLM Guide

This file gives implementation-accurate guidance for coding agents using `@spkm/ui`.

## 1) Install and Import

```bash
bun add @spkm/ui
```

```tsx
import '@spkm/ui/styles.css'
import { AppSidebar, AgentChat } from '@spkm/ui'
```

Stylesheet import is required. If missing, components look broken.

## 2) What This Package Owns

- Sparkmate visual tokens and utility classes
- App shell UI primitives
- AI-core styled chat UI component
- Full shadcn/ui component exports

## 3) What Consumer App Owns

- Router integration and navigation actions
- Auth and session management
- Data fetching / API / state orchestration
- Nav configuration values (`NavGroup[]`)

Do not assume package ships default app routes.

## 4) Sidebar Usage

`AppSidebar` props:
- `groups: NavGroup[]` (required)
- `pathname: string` (required)
- `onNavigate: (path: string) => void` (required)
- `user: NavUserInfo` (required)
- optional:
  - `onProfileClick`
  - `onSignOut`
  - `logoSrc`
  - `logoIconSrc`
  - `afterNavMain`
  - `beforeNavUser`

`NavGroup`:
- `title: string`
- `path?: string`
- `icon?: LucideIcon`
- `imageSrc?: string`
- `imageAlt?: string`
- `items?: NavItem[]`

Use icon or image for top-level nav item visuals.

## 5) Chat Usage

Use `AgentChat` for AI-core style chat frame.

Main props:
- controlled:
  - `messages?: AgentChatMessage[]`
  - `onMessagesChange?: (messages) => void`
- uncontrolled:
  - `defaultMessages?: AgentChatMessage[]`
- submit pipeline:
  - `onSubmit?: (text) => AgentChatMessage | AgentChatMessage[] | Promise<...>`
- visual config:
  - `assistantName`
  - `assistantAvatarSrc`
  - `modelLabel`
  - `emptyTitle`
  - `emptyDescription`
  - `placeholder`
- behavior:
  - `showClear`
  - `onClear`
  - `showClose`
  - `onClose`
  - `disabled`

`AgentChat` already handles:
- Enter to send, Shift+Enter newline
- auto-resize input
- auto-scroll
- "New chat" clear action
- empty state suggestions

## 6) Canonical Integration Example

```tsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import {
  AgentChat,
  AppSidebar,
  SidebarInset,
  SidebarProvider,
  type AgentChatMessage,
  type NavGroup,
} from '@spkm/ui'
import '@spkm/ui/styles.css'

const groups: NavGroup[] = [
  {
    title: 'Chat',
    path: '/chat',
    imageSrc: '/Kim.png',
    imageAlt: 'Kim',
    items: [],
  },
]

export function AppShell() {
  const [pathname, setPathname] = useState('/chat')
  const [messages, setMessages] = useState<AgentChatMessage[]>([])

  return (
    <SidebarProvider>
      <AppSidebar
        groups={groups}
        pathname={pathname}
        onNavigate={setPathname}
        user={{ name: 'Sparkmate User', email: 'user@sparkmate.com' }}
      />
      <SidebarInset className="p-4">
        <AgentChat
          messages={messages}
          onMessagesChange={setMessages}
          assistantName="Kim"
          assistantAvatarSrc="/Kim.png"
          modelLabel="GPT-5.1"
          onSubmit={async (text) => ({
            role: 'assistant',
            content: `Response for: ${text}`,
          })}
        />
      </SidebarInset>
    </SidebarProvider>
  )
}
```

## 7) Do / Don't

Do:
- import `@spkm/ui/styles.css`
- pass explicit `groups`, `pathname`, `onNavigate`
- keep package components presentational

Don't:
- assume built-in router bindings
- assume built-in auth provider
- hardcode app-specific nav in package layer

## 8) Source of Truth

- Public exports: `src/index.ts`
- App shell examples: `playground/src/App.tsx`
- Consumer contract: `docs/CONSUMER-CONTRACT.md`
